page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. This server is configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. The CPU allocation is shared. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

In standardized benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places this instance in the top tier (top 10%) with a score of 3138.07. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the average tier (middle 50%) with a score of 25024.21. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 29.39 GB/sec, while single-threaded and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieve 13.4 GB/sec and 53.07 GB/sec respectively, all aligning with average tier performance.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core workloads despite the high-performing single-core metrics. However, the generous allocation of 8.0 GB of RAM per core and the substantial 1920 GB local NVMe SSD storage offer high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. This hardware profile is well-suited for medium-sized databases, caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose applications requiring large local storage capacities.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC Milan processor with an x86_64 architecture and a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 3138.07, while its multi-core stress-ng score of 25024.21 and bzip3 decompression metrics fall within the average tier. The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained multi-core workloads, but the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and large local storage capacity deliver strong qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense applications. This configuration is suitable for database hosting, memory-intensive data processing, and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server combining top-tier single-core performance with a substantial local NVMe SSD for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
1920 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
